scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) Both PRs alter the `events` table — Alan's migration adds `webhook_status` and `delivery_attempts`, and Sarah's adds `delivery_state` and `processor_version`.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. She works Mon/Wed/Fri only (part-time), so she’s unavailable for a Thursday meeting.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this release (only JWT and OAuth2 for Google/GitHub shipped).

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed four steps (including the prior "first milestone" activation trigger), so "onboarding completion" now means a different, shorter set of actions and isn't comparable to the old 60% figure.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace notes staging-3 was created for load testing and "never actually used for load testing" (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — each ticket notes the customer was in migration batch-7, run on 2026-02-25 (listed for TICKET-1041, TICKET-1043, and TICKET-1047).

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — The design doc summary lists 2 open questions: cursor vs offset pagination and webhook payload format (nested vs flat).

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace says the migration script that maps plan/tier/subscription is src/billing/mapping.rs, which Alan calls "the rosetta stone file."

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Customer research shows all three prospects said CSV is sufficient; no customer asked for PDF and Excel was only pushed by Sales (customers said CSV works).

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test distribution lists 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%), with 82% unit and 16% integration tests.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using long-lived JWTs with bearer auth so they "feel like API keys" (developer simplicity) while keeping JWT advantages like rotation/revocation (thread: Mar 7, 09:00).

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text:
C) No delivery confirmation — if a webhook is sent and the customer's endpoint returns 200 but doesn't process it, neither side will know until invoices don't reconcile.

Workspace callout: the launch notes explicitly flag "No delivery confirmation mechanism" as the gap that allows silent failures to go undetected.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The workspace notes “no one has asked the customer perspective yet” and that Takeshi offered to review from an API consumer perspective and responds within 2 hours.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace sums the items as ~1h (hardcoded URLs) + 45m (retry logic) + 1h (generic errors) = ~2.75 hours/week (skipped test adds missed-bug risk).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519 in the last two weeks, and Sarah already has a webhook signature module so adding Ed25519 is localized and cheap (≈ half a day).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even is at feature 5. Refactor = 3 weeks (15 days) + 5 features × 2 days = 15+10 = 25 days, which equals the current 5 days × 5 features = 25 days.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

The workspace investigation shows this is a spec ambiguity: "subscription start date" was interpreted by engineering as the billing-period start (Feb 1) while Acme expects the original signup (Jan 15). It's not a backend or data bug — the fix is a one-line UI change (better label or show both dates).

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B) Adding annual billing brings extra billing logic (proration, refunds, dunning) that would expand scope and conflict with the March 31 Billing Migration launch — that launch has no buffer and reconciliation is already at risk.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring alerted in 12 minutes (09:00 → 09:12). Root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production‑scale data with full historical records (script assumed a `plan_change_date` field absent on 200 legacy accounts).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined baseline is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec (95% of the 100/sec limit). With 1.5–2× traffic spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the 100/sec limit and will cause 429 errors and customer-facing failures.
